subject: [PATCH v4 03/14] mm: rename uffd-wp PTE accessors to uffd

reviews.author: Andrew Morton
reviews.summary: Raised concerns about addressing existing issues before adding new features to the patch series. Suggested that it would be better to address these issues immediately and then base the new feature-add on top.
reviews.raw_body:
On Mon, 25 May 2026 15:43:32 +0100 Kiryl Shutsemau <kirill@shutemov.name> wrote:

> To: sashiko-reviews@lists.linux.dev

hm.  What's this.

Sashiko seems to have been useful this time around.

> Confirmed and pre-existing ... Will address in a separate
> patch on top of this series.

There are quite a few like this.  Would it be better to address these
existing issues immediately then base the new feature-add on top?

reviews.author: Kiryl Shutsemau
reviews.summary: Confirmed that the non-huge sibling make_uffd_wp_pte() uses ptep_modify_prot_start() correctly and agreed to address this in a separate patch on top of the series.
reviews.raw_body:
On Mon, May 25, 2026 at 12:31:08PM -0700, Andrew Morton wrote:
> On Mon, 25 May 2026 15:43:32 +0100 Kiryl Shutsemau <kirill@shutemov.name> wrote:
> 
> > To: sashiko-reviews@lists.linux.dev
> 
> hm.  What's this.

kvm@ folks opted-in for getting review feedback by mail for patchsets
sent to the list. I've replied to all recipients of the patchset not
only to kvm@.

> Sashiko seems to have been useful this time around.

Yeah, better than last time.

> > Confirmed and pre-existing ... Will address in a separate
> > patch on top of this series.
> 
> There are quite a few like this.  Would it be better to address these
> existing issues immediately then base the new feature-add on top?

Fair enough. Will prepare patches this week.

-- 
  Kiryl Shutsemau / Kirill A. Shutemov

2026-05-25.patch_summary: The patch to rename uffd-wp PTE accessors to uffd has been confirmed by Kiryl Shutsemau, but Andrew Morton suggests addressing existing issues before adding new features.
